stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
Key technologies mentioned include transformers, safetensors, and the Llama architecture.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags such as 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The source, Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicates relevance within the open-source AI community.

stories.title: Nearly a million passports and photo IDs were left unprotected on the public internet
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: A significant data breach has exposed nearly a million identity documents (passports and photo IDs) online.
The vulnerability stems from documents being left unprotected and accessible via simple web searches.
This highlights critical security risks associated with personal data exposure on the public internet.

stories.title: Man sues Florida cops over arrest spurred by "93% match" in facial recognition
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: A lawsuit challenges the use of facial recognition technology by Florida police.
The arrest in question was reportedly based on a '93% match' generated by the AI system.
The core legal argument questions the reliance on an 'error-prone AI system' in law enforcement.

stories.title: Google DeepMind releases DiffusionGemma, a model that runs local AI 4x faster
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google DeepMind has released DiffusionGemma, a new AI model.
The model leverages Diffusion AI technology to significantly accelerate text generation.
DiffusionGemma is notable for enabling local AI execution up to four times faster.

stories.title: Nobody needs AI to search the Internet, court says in ruling against Google
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Google's AI Overview faced a legal setback in a German court ruling.
The ruling suggests that AI is not a necessary component for internet searching, challenging current AI search trends.
This legal outcome could significantly impact the trajectory and viability of the AI search industry.

stories.title: Which Models Are Our Models Built On? Auditing Invisible Dependencies in Modern LLMs
stories.author: Sewon Min
bullet_points: Modern LLM pipelines exhibit complex, recursive dependencies on other models for data generation, filtering, and evaluation.
The full dependency structure is fragmented across various public artifacts, making manual tracing nearly impossible due to high complexity.
The paper introduces 'ModSleuth,' a tool designed to audit and trace these invisible, heterogeneous dependencies within LLM ecosystems.

stories.title: German court ruling declares Google's AI Overviews are Google's own words and makes it liable for false answers
stories.author: the-decoder.com via gspr
bullet_points: A German court ruled that Google's AI Overviews are considered Google's own generated content.
The ruling holds Google legally liable for any false or inaccurate answers provided by its AI Overviews.
This decision represents a significant legal challenge to the liability framework of generative AI models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on the Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, indicating a focus on practical LLM deployment.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The associated pipeline is focused on text-generation tasks.
Key technologies involved include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, promising faster local AI inference.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects these apps to local AI coding agents.
A key feature is that all AI processing runs entirely on the user's local device for privacy.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is the simplified setup, requiring no environment variables or config files.

stories.title: FACTR 2: Learning External Force Sensing for Commodity Robot Arms Improves Policy Learning
stories.author: Deepak Pathak
bullet_points: Introduces NEXT, a data-driven method to estimate external joint torques in robot arms without dedicated force sensors.
The method is highly efficient, training in just 1 minute using only 10 minutes of free-motion data.
This breakthrough enables force-feedback teleoperation and improves policy learning on low-cost robotic hardware.

stories.title: TAHOE: Text-to-SQL with Automated Hint Optimization from Experience
stories.author: Peng Li
bullet_points: Introduces 'Tahoe,' a system designed to improve Text-to-SQL performance in real-world production environments.
Addresses key challenges in Text-to-SQL deployment, such as handling strict SQL dialects, massive schemas, and the cost/rigidity of traditional fine-tuning.
Tahoe employs an error-driven hint learning pipeline, treating prompt optimization as a dynamic data management problem.

stories.title: FACTR 2: Learning External Force Sensing for Commodity Robot Arms Improves Policy Learning
stories.author: Deepak Pathak
bullet_points: Introduces NEXT, a data-driven method to estimate external joint torques in robot arms without dedicated, costly force sensors.
The method trains very quickly (1 minute) using minimal free-motion data (10 minutes) while matching the performance of dedicated sensors.
This innovation enables force-feedback teleoperation and improves policy learning for low-cost robotic systems, addressing a major hardware limitation.

stories.title: On Subquadratic Architectures: From Applications to Principles
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The paper addresses the computational bottleneck of quadratic attention in dominant Transformer models.
It compares three leading subquadratic architectures: xLSTM, Mamba-2, and Gated DeltaNet.
Evaluation focuses on complex sequence modeling tasks, including code-model pre-training and distillation.

stories.title: ALIGNBEAM : Inference-Time Alignment Transfer via Cross-Vocabulary Logit Mixing
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: Addresses a critical safety issue where domain fine-tuning degrades LLM safety, causing specialists to comply with harmful prompts.
Introduces ALIGNBEAM, a training-free method to enhance safety by mixing logits from a safe anchor model.
Overcomes a major limitation of existing defenses by translating anchor logits into the target model's vocabulary, enabling cross-family specialist protection.

stories.title: Google ADK Security: 5 Layers That Defend AI Agents From Prompt Injection
stories.author: ainaomotayo
bullet_points: The article addresses a critical security vulnerability: Prompt Injection targeting AI agents.
It details a multi-layered defense mechanism (5 layers) within Google ADK to mitigate these attacks.
The content uses a high-impact anecdote (unauthorized $3,000 refund) to illustrate the real-world risk.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden the range of supported models across various hardware.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, particularly boosting support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes local-first operation, allowing AI to run entirely on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to run tools like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary APIs like Anthropic's.

stories.title: Deezer launches an AI music detector for other streaming services
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Deezer has launched an AI music detector capable of scanning playlists from competing streaming services.
Deezer was an early mover in labeling AI-generated music, though its technology adoption by other platforms was slow.
The development highlights the growing industry challenge of identifying and managing AI-generated content across music streaming platforms.

stories.title: Xbox warns of a ‘reset’ as it prepares for layoffs
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Xbox division is reportedly facing significant layoffs next month.
Microsoft has been internally preparing for these workforce reductions for several weeks.
The cuts may involve studio closures or other major organizational changes.

stories.title: Apple, Google add support for Thread 1.4
stories.author: Jennifer Pattison Tuohy
bullet_points: Apple and Google are updating their smart home streaming devices to support Thread 1.4.
The update is visible in the tvOS 27 developer beta for Apple TVs and via a software update for Google TV Streamers.
This advancement supports the growing ecosystem of Matter smart home standards.

stories.title: Kalshi adds required employment verification for some prediction market bets
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: The CFTC is considering its first regulation specifically for prediction markets.
The regulatory push is driven by increasing arrests related to 'insider trading' in these markets.
The proposed rule aims to create a framework to determine if prediction market contracts fall under existing financial regulations.

stories.title: Claude Fable won’t answer basic biology questions
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, claiming it is their most powerful widely available AI model.
Despite promotional claims of advanced capabilities, the model fails to answer basic biology questions.
The model defaults to handing off simple queries rather than providing direct answers.

stories.title: Reroute, Don't Remove: Recoverable Visual Token Routing for Vision-Language Models
stories.author: Yu-Lun Liu
bullet_points: Addresses the computational bottleneck in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) caused by high-dimensional visual tokens.
Proposes 'Recoverable Visual Token Routing' as an alternative to existing 'rank-and-remove' methods.
Highlights the flaw in current token reduction techniques: the permanent discarding of tokens whose importance changes during decoder inference.

stories.title: DIRECT: When and Where Should You Allocate Test-Time Compute in Embodied Planners?
stories.author: Marco Pavone
bullet_points: The paper addresses the current trend of scaling test-time compute in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) used for embodied agents.
Excessive compute scaling leads to increased latency, token usage, and FLOPs without proportional gains in real-world success.
The core argument is that strategically deciding *when* and *where* to allocate compute is crucial for practical deployment of frontier AI models.

stories.title: Redesign Mixture-of-Experts Routers with Manifold Power Iteration
stories.author: Yankai Lin
bullet_points: The paper addresses a fundamental design challenge in Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models: improving the router component.
The router's role is to select relevant experts based on input token similarity, but current designs lack principled ways to encode expert information.
The proposed solution involves using Manifold Power Iteration to redesign and enhance the MoE routers.

stories.title: ATLAS: Active Theory Learning for Automated Science
stories.author: Kevin J. Miller
bullet_points: Introduces ATLAS, an active learning framework designed to automate scientific discovery.
ATLAS focuses on generating interpretable, mechanistic behavioral models using data-driven methods.
The framework iterates by generating hypotheses represented as an ensemble of sparse neural networks (Disentangled RNNs).

stories.title: APPO: Agentic Procedural Policy Optimization
stories.author: Xiangxiang Chu
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in current agentic Reinforcement Learning (RL) methods where credit assignment is too coarse.
The work focuses on two key areas: determining optimal branching points within agent workflows and refining credit assignment after branching.
It aims to improve the ability of LLM agents to accurately attribute influence to specific intermediate decisions during multi-turn tool use.

stories.title: Illumination-Robust Camera-Based Heart-Rate Estimation for Physiological Sensing in Robots
stories.author: Torbjörn E. M. Nordling
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of illumination variation in remote photoplethysmography (rPPG) for heart-rate estimation.
Proposes an end-to-end spatial-temporal transformer framework to improve robustness.
Relevant for advancing physiological sensing capabilities in service and assistive robots.

stories.title: Context-Driven Incremental Compression for Multi-Turn Dialogue Generation
stories.author: Lei Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the computational burden of long dialogue histories in conversational AI due to growing attention costs.
Proposes a novel 'Context-Driven Incremental Compression' method to manage dialogue context efficiently.
Highlights limitations in existing context compressors, specifically regarding cross-turn memory sharing and error compounding in long conversations.

stories.title: Redesign Mixture-of-Experts Routers with Manifold Power Iteration
stories.author: Yankai Lin
bullet_points: The research focuses on improving the 'Router' component within Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models, a critical part of modern large language models.
The core problem addressed is the lack of design principles to effectively encode expert information into the router's representative vectors.
The proposed solution involves using 'Manifold Power Iteration' to redesign and enhance the MoE router mechanism.

stories.title: ATLAS: Active Theory Learning for Automated Science
stories.author: Kevin J. Miller
bullet_points: Introduces ATLAS, an active learning framework designed to automate scientific discovery.
ATLAS focuses on generating interpretable, mechanistic behavioral models from data.
The framework iteratively generates hypotheses using an ensemble of sparse neural networks (Disentangled RNNs).

stories.title: APPO: Agentic Procedural Policy Optimization
stories.author: Xiangxiang Chu
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in agentic Reinforcement Learning (RL) regarding credit assignment in multi-turn tool-use by LLM agents.
The core focus is on two problems: determining optimal branching points within agent workflows and accurately assigning credit after those branches.
This work aims to provide finer-grained analysis of intermediate decision-making processes that influence final outcomes in complex AI agents.

stories.title: UniIntervene: Agentic Intervention for Efficient Real-World Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Ziwei Wang
bullet_points: Introduces UniIntervene, a new agentic intervention model for Human-in-the-loop Reinforcement Learning (HiL-RL).
Addresses the high labor cost and scalability issues of current HiL-RL frameworks, which require frequent human corrections.
Aims to improve real-world robotic manipulation by enabling more efficient policy improvement with less human intervention.

stories.title: Breaking Entropy Bounds: Accelerating RL Training via MTP with Rejection Sampling
stories.author: Jingren Zhou
bullet_points: The paper addresses the bottleneck in Reinforcement Learning (RL) training for LLMs, specifically during the rollout stage.
It focuses on Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) and the issue of declining acceptance rates during RL training, which limits speedup.
The authors introduce 'Bebop,' a systematic study aimed at improving MTP performance within LLM post-training pipelines.

stories.title: Latent World Recovery for Multimodal Learning with Missing Modalities
stories.author: Simon McDade
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of multimodal learning when some data modalities are missing.
Proposes Latent World Recovery (LWR), a framework for handling incomplete multimodal data.
The method aligns modality-specific embeddings in a shared latent space to construct unified representations.

stories.title: Anatomy of Post-Training: Using Interpretability to Characterize Data and Shape the Learning Signal
stories.author: Ekdeep Singh Lubana
bullet_points: The paper addresses the lack of visibility into how data shapes language model behavior during post-training.
Current training methods rely on scalar rewards, which abstract away the diverse lessons present in the preference data.
The research proposes using interpretability techniques to inspect preference datasets *before* optimization to mitigate issues like spurious correlations and sycophancy.

stories.title: Doc-to-Atom: Learning to Compile and Compose Memory Atoms
stories.author: Srinivas Chappidi
bullet_points: Addresses the quadratic complexity and memory cost of attention in LLMs when handling long input sequences.
Proposes 'Doc-to-Atom' to compile and compose 'Memory Atoms' for efficient document understanding.
Builds upon context distillation techniques, aiming to improve upon existing methods like Doc-to-LoRA.

stories.title: Redesign Mixture-of-Experts Routers with Manifold Power Iteration
stories.author: Yankai Lin
bullet_points: The research addresses a fundamental design challenge in Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models: optimizing the router component.
The proposed method utilizes Manifold Power Iteration to redesign the MoE routers.
The goal is to improve how router rows encode expert information to better reflect token-expert affinity.

stories.title: Verifiable Environments Are LEGO Bricks: Recursive Composition for Reasoning Generalization
stories.author: Dayiheng Liu
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitation of linear scaling in using verifiable environments to enhance LLM reasoning via Reinforcement Learning (RL).
The paper introduces RACES, a method designed for Recursive Automated Composition, to overcome the scaling limits of manual environment construction.
The work focuses on improving reasoning generalization in LLMs by leveraging the compositional nature of verifiable environments.

stories.title: Breaking Entropy Bounds: Accelerating RL Training via MTP with Rejection Sampling
stories.author: Jingren Zhou
bullet_points: The research addresses the bottleneck in Reinforcement Learning (RL) training for LLMs, specifically during the rollout stage.
It focuses on Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) and its degradation in acceptance rates during RL training, which limits speedup.
The authors introduce 'Bebop,' a systematic study designed to improve MTP performance in LLM post-training.

stories.title: Claw-SWE-Bench: A Benchmark for Evaluating OpenClaw-style Agent Harnesses on Coding Tasks
stories.author: Yu Wang
bullet_points: Introduces Claw-SWE-Bench, a new benchmark designed to evaluate open-source agent harnesses (like OpenClaw) on coding tasks.
Addresses a limitation in existing benchmarks (like SWE-bench) where generic agents struggle to meet strict execution contracts (Docker workspace, patching).
Provides a standardized, multilingual protocol to fairly compare different heterogeneous agent harnesses under controlled conditions.

stories.title: Pokémon Go Scans Trained the Navigation Tech for Military Drones
stories.author: vrganj
bullet_points: Pokémon Go's location-based scanning technology was repurposed for military drone navigation.
This demonstrates the transferability of consumer-grade AR/location tech to defense applications.
The story highlights the intersection of gaming technology and advanced military hardware.

stories.title: Anthropic requires 30 day data retention for Fable and Mythos
stories.author: lebovic
bullet_points: Anthropic mandates a 30-day data retention policy for its Fable and Mythos models.
The news relates to data governance and privacy within advanced AI models.
The context suggests internal or restricted access to these specific Anthropic/Microsoft-related AI features.
